Motion and appearance information are extracted separately in the second category approach, requiring different modules for each. Extracting only appearance features from a single frame may fail to capture the correspondence of appearance information between frames.

The proposed approach in the paper involves explicitly extracting both motion and appearance information through a unified operation of inter-frame attention. This method aims to enhance appearance features between consecutive frames while acquiring motion features at the same time using attention maps.

The unified operation of inter-frame attention is utilized to extract motion features along with appearance features. By reusing attention maps, the method can enhance appearance features between frames and acquire motion features efficiently in a single step.

The basic processing unit of inter-frame attention can be stacked to obtain hierarchical motion and appearance information. This allows for a more detailed analysis of the relationship between appearance and motion features across frames.

By stacking the basic processing unit of inter-frame attention, hierarchical motion and appearance information can be obtained. This hierarchical approach enables the extraction of more complex motion features along with appearance features.
